pi_items.source: NCBI Bookshelf StatPearls and Pfizer official labeling
NCBI Bookshelf StatPearls - pediatric dosing range is 2-20 mcg/kg/min with initial dose of 5 mcg/kg/min; 10 is standard maintenance dose
Drugs.com and ASHP guidelines - 2000 mcg/mL (2 mg/mL) is a standard dobutamine concentration preparation
Task specification - 12.5 kg is within pediatric range (neonates to 18 years); verifier accepts 10-15 kg range
Task specification - tolerance parameter for weight setting
Task specification - file path where results are written

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring a simulated OpenICE medical infusion system with pediatric dosing parameters. Dobutamine is a real inotropic drug used in clinical practice. The dose of 10 mcg/kg/min is within the standard pediatric dosing range (2-20 mcg/kg/min) recommended for children and is commonly used as a maintenance dose. The concentration of 2000 mcg/mL (2 mg/mL) is a standard preparation concentration for dobutamine infusions. The target weight of 12.5 kg represents a typical pediatric patient. The expected infusion rate calculated by the formula (Dose × Weight × 60) / Concentration equals 3.75 mL/hr, which is the mathematically correct flow rate for these parameters.
